selected_important_turn.text: I am flexible, but I would prefer some food and firewood.  I have a large family to feed and all of my food needs to be cooked.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the first substantive disclosure of your needs and willingness to cooperate. It shapes the space for integrative trades by signaling flexibility and giving a reason (large family / cooking) that could be used to design mutually beneficial exchanges.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although it signals prosocial intent, it is vague: it doesn't specify quantities, priorities (exactly how much food vs firewood), essential minimums, or what you might trade away. That vagueness leaves potential value on the table and makes it harder for the opponent to craft a high-quality, fair package that meets your most pressing needs.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Be more specific and strategic while staying cooperative: state target quantities or minimums (e.g., how many firewood you need at least, how much food is essential), rank between food and firewood if one is more critical, and/or offer a concrete trade-off you’re willing to make. Alternatively, ask a clarifying question about their constraints so you can propose an integrated package.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — clearer priorities enable trades that create more total value and reduce wasted concessions.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — explicit minimums and priorities help produce an allocation that better matches each side's needs.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — clearer numbers reduce back-and-forth and speed toward a concrete proposal.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: slight increase — specificity combined with continued flexibility signals trustworthiness and competence.

value2reason.Medium: I am very thirsty but not dying yet.
value2reason.High: I haven't eaten in three days.

value2reason.Low: I already have 4 cases of 48 bottles of water - sufficient for everyone to have 10 bottles.
value2reason.Medium: My entire family is coming on the camping trip and I don't have enough to feed them all, else my kids will only be eating marshmallows.
value2reason.High: I don't have any means to cook my food, which is predominantly raw meat.  The forecast is also going to be cold weather and I need a source of heat.

dialogue.text: Hello, i will offer you as much food as you want for all of your fire wood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hello, how are you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Im good how are you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm doing great, thanks.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: How many of each supply would you like? what is your favorite?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I am flexible, but I would prefer some food and firewood.  I have a large family to feed and all of my food needs to be cooked.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand. Since you need food for your family, I'll offer you 2 Food and 1 Firewood in exchange for 1 Water. This way, I get the Water I need, and you get more Food and Firewood to cook for your family.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 Firewood packages to cook for my family and 2 Food packages to feed everyone. I’m willing to trade 1 Water for 1 Firewood or 1 Food. Let’s find a fair split that works for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m happy to adjust. How about this: I’ll give you 2 Food and 1 Firewood for 1 Water. That way, you get enough to cook and feed your family, and I get the Water I need. Let me know if that works for you.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your needs. Let's finalize this: I'll give you 2 Food and 1 Firewood for 1 Water. That covers your requirements, and I get the Water I need. Are you okay with this split?

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 Firewood and 2 Food packages to meet my family's needs. If you're okay with trading 1 Water for 1 Firewood or 1 Food, we can finalize this split. Let me know your preference.
